gateRules.description: Structural load-bearing requirements for log construction
gateRules.trigger: log cabin construction, notching, wall assembly, roof framing
gateRules.action: GATE -- confirm load path from roof to foundation; saddle notches must seat fully (no gaps); green wood shrinks -- plan for settling; do not use rotted or checked logs for structural members

gateRules.description: Structural integrity for any shelter frame
gateRules.trigger: any shelter construction (wigwam, longhouse, tupiq, dome)
gateRules.action: GATE -- verify frame stability before adding covering; pole joints must resist lateral wind load; cover material must not add unexpected point loads to frame

annotateRules.description: Fire safety for stone chimney and hearth construction
annotateRules.trigger: stone chimney, fireplace, hearth, open fire in shelter
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- stone chimney requires proper flue sizing; firebox must be isolated from combustible framing with non-combustible material; maintain clearances from wood framing (minimum 2 inches); cap chimney to prevent moisture entry and animal ingress

annotateRules.description: CO awareness for enclosed shelter fire
annotateRules.trigger: fire, stove, or heat source in any enclosed shelter
annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- any combustion source in an enclosed shelter produces CO; ensure adequate ventilation; this is a high-level awareness note -- see Room 14 (Arctic Living) for full CO safety protocol in igloo and enclosed snow shelter scenarios

safetyNotes.structuralSettling: Green (freshly cut) logs shrink and settle as they dry. Log cabin builders must account for settling above doors and windows with 'settling spaces' filled with insulation. Failure to account for settling can cause doors to bind or structural racking.
safetyNotes.notchIntegrity: A saddle notch must bear the full weight of the log above it. The saddle must seat the lower log fully -- no rocking or gaps. A log that can rock on its notch will eventually fail under load or frost heaving.
safetyNotes.fireSafety: Stone chimney construction requires understanding of flue draft, firebox geometry, and isolation of combustible framing. See a qualified mason before constructing a functional chimney.
